title: Heredity
summary: Chapter 8 introduces heredity — the passing of traits from parents to offspring. Mendel's experiments with pea plants established the laws of inheritance. The concepts of dominant and recessive traits, genotype, phenotype, homozygous, and heterozygous are explained. The Punnett square is used to predict offspring ratios. Sex determination in humans is explained through sex chromosomes (XX = female, XY = male). The role of DNA and genes in controlling characteristics is covered.
learningPoints: Explain Mendel's experiments: trait selection, monohybrid and dihybrid crosses
Define dominant, recessive, homozygous, heterozygous, genotype, phenotype
State Mendel's Laws: Law of Segregation, Law of Independent Assortment
Use Punnett square to predict offspring ratios
Monohybrid cross: F1 all dominant; F2 ratio 3:1 (phenotypic), 1:2:1 (genotypic)
Explain sex determination in humans: XX (female), XY (male)
Explain how the father determines sex of child
Define gene as a segment of DNA that controls a specific characteristic
Distinguish between inherited and acquired traits

laws.name: Mendel's Law of Segregation
laws.statement: During gamete formation, alleles of each gene pair separate (segregate) so that each gamete carries only one allele for each gene.

laws.name: Mendel's Law of Independent Assortment
laws.statement: Genes for different traits segregate independently of each other during gamete formation (applicable to genes on different chromosomes).

formulas.label: Monohybrid F2 Ratio
formulas.expression: Phenotypic: 3 dominant : 1 recessive | Genotypic: 1 TT : 2 Tt : 1 tt
formulas.description: F1 cross Tt × Tt gives 3:1 phenotypic and 1:2:1 genotypic ratios.

formulas.label: Dihybrid F2 Ratio
formulas.description: TTRR × ttrr → F1 TtRr → F2 shows 9:3:3:1 ratio.

formulas.label: Sex Chromosomes
formulas.expression: Female: XX | Male: XY | Offspring: 50% XX (girl) : 50% XY (boy)
formulas.description: Father determines sex — he provides either X or Y chromosome.

diagrams.title: Monohybrid Punnett Square
diagrams.description: Tt × Tt: TT, Tt, Tt, tt → 3 tall : 1 dwarf. 1:2:1 genotypic ratio.

diagrams.title: Sex Determination in Humans
diagrams.description: Father (XY) → X or Y gametes. Mother (XX) → X gametes only. XX = girl, XY = boy. Father determines sex of child.

resources.label: Karnataka SSLC Science Textbook
resources.description: Official Karnataka SSLC Science textbook Chapter 8 Heredity.

resources.label: NCERT Science Textbook Class 10
resources.description: NCERT Class 10 Science Chapter 9 Heredity and Evolution.

resources.label: Heredity — Video Lessons
resources.description: Khan Academy videos on Mendel's experiments, genetics and sex determination.

pyqs.question: In a zygote there are 24 pairs of chromosomes. Number of chromosomes in gametes: (A) 24 pairs (B) 48 (C) 6 pairs (D) 24
pyqs.answer: (D) 24. Gametes are haploid (n). 24 pairs = 48 chromosomes. Gametes have 48÷2 = 24 chromosomes.

pyqs.question: Red flowering pea plant (RR) crossed with white (rr). Show F2 with checker board. Write phenotypic and genotypic ratios.
pyqs.answer: F1: all Rr (red). F2 cross Rr × Rr: RR, Rr, Rr, rr. Phenotypic: 3 red : 1 white. Genotypic: 1 RR : 2 Rr : 1 rr.

pyqs.question: Explain determination of sex of male child in humans.
pyqs.answer: Father has XY, mother has XX. Father produces X or Y sperm; mother produces only X eggs. XX → girl, XY → boy. Father determines sex of child — 50% chance each.

pyqs.question: DNA replication definition.
pyqs.answer: DNA replication is the process by which the mother cell's DNA copies itself during the preparatory phase of cell division (S phase of interphase).

pyqs.question: Correct statements about gene: (A) unit of DNA (B) source of information for protein (C) single gene synthesises several proteins (D) functions as enzyme. Answer: (A) (i) and (ii) only
pyqs.answer: (B) statements (i) and (ii) only. A gene is a unit of DNA that carries information for making a specific protein. One gene = one protein (generally).

pyqs.question: Dihybrid cross: Red tall (TTRR) × white dwarf (ttrr). Show F2 and write ratio.
pyqs.answer: F1: all TtRr (tall red). F2 cross TtRr × TtRr using 4×4 Punnett square: 9 Tall Red : 3 Tall White : 3 Dwarf Red : 1 Dwarf White = 9:3:3:1.

mcqs.question: Phenotypic ratio in F2 generation of monohybrid cross is:
Genotypic ratio in F2 of monohybrid cross is:
mcqs.explanation: Tt × Tt → TT : 2Tt : tt = 1:2:1. TT and Tt look same (dominant phenotype) but have different genotypes.

mcqs.question: Sex of child is determined by:
options: B. Father's X or Y chromosome
mcqs.explanation: Mother provides only X. Father provides X (→girl) or Y (→boy). Father determines sex. XX = girl, XY = boy.

mcqs.question: Dihybrid cross F2 phenotypic ratio is:
mcqs.explanation: TtRr × TtRr → 9 T_R_ : 3 T_rr : 3 ttR_ : 1 ttrr = 9:3:3:1.

mcqs.question: A gene is:
options: C. A unit of DNA carrying information
mcqs.explanation: A gene is a specific sequence of DNA that codes for a specific protein/trait. Genes are located on chromosomes.

mcqs.question: In Mendel's experiments, the trait that appeared in F1 generation was called:
mcqs.explanation: Dominant trait appears in F1 (heterozygous). Recessive trait is masked in F1 but reappears in F2 (1/4 of offspring).

mcqs.question: Chromosomes of female humans are:
mcqs.explanation: Human females have XX sex chromosomes. Males have XY. The Y chromosome carries genes for male characteristics.

mcqs.question: Indicator of trait having green seeds (G) and flowers at top of stem (a) is:
mcqs.explanation: G = green seeds (dominant), a = top of stem (recessive). GGaa (homozygous dominant green, homozygous recessive top) or Ggaa.

mcqs.question: Height of a plant is determined by genes because:
options: A. Genes control all traits directly
B. Genes secrete specific enzymes that control growth
D. Genes control cell size only
mcqs.explanation: Genes secrete specific enzymes. If enzyme works efficiently, plant grows taller. Gene → enzyme → trait.

mcqs.question: Law of segregation states that:
options: B. Alleles separate during gamete formation
C. Dominant allele always suppresses recessive
mcqs.explanation: During meiosis, the two alleles of each gene segregate into different gametes. Each gamete carries one allele.

mcqs.question: Acquired traits are:
options: B. Traits developed during lifetime, not inherited
mcqs.explanation: Acquired traits develop due to environment/use during lifetime and are NOT passed to offspring (e.g., body building muscles, learned skills).

mcqs.question: Tall pea plant (TT) crossed with dwarf (tt). All F1 plants are:
mcqs.explanation: TT × tt → all Tt. Since T is dominant, all F1 are tall. Tt heterozygous shows dominant phenotype.

mcqs.question: The F2 ratio in dihybrid cross shows:
mcqs.explanation: 9 tall+round : 3 tall+wrinkled : 3 dwarf+round : 1 dwarf+wrinkled = 4 distinct phenotype classes.

mcqs.question: DNA is found in:
mcqs.explanation: DNA is packaged in chromosomes inside the nucleus. Humans have 46 chromosomes (23 pairs). Each chromosome = one long DNA molecule.

mcqs.question: Mendel chose pea plants for his experiments because:
options: A. Pea plants are easy to grow
B. Pea plants show clearly distinguishable traits and can be self/cross pollinated
mcqs.explanation: Pea plants have 7 pairs of clearly contrasting traits, short life cycle, easy to control pollination (self or cross), and large number of offspring.

exercises.question: Show F1 and F2 generations when tall (TT) pea plant is crossed with dwarf (tt). Write phenotypic and genotypic ratios.
exercises.answer: F1: TT × tt → all Tt (all tall). F1 × F1: Tt × Tt → TT : 2Tt : tt = 1:2:1 genotypic. Phenotypic: 3 tall : 1 dwarf (3:1).

exercises.question: Explain sex determination in humans with a diagram.
exercises.answer: Father XY, Mother XX. Meiosis: Father → X or Y sperm; Mother → only X eggs. XX combination = girl (50% chance); XY combination = boy (50% chance). Father determines sex. Society blaming mother for sex of child is scientifically wrong.

exercises.question: Distinguish between dominant and recessive traits.
exercises.answer: Dominant trait: expressed in both homozygous (TT) and heterozygous (Tt) condition. Recessive trait: expressed only in homozygous condition (tt), masked by dominant allele in Tt. In F1 cross, dominant appears; in F2, recessive reappears (1 in 4 offspring).

exercises.question: Show dihybrid cross with F2 generation. Write F2 ratio.
exercises.answer: TTRR (tall round) × ttrr (dwarf wrinkled) → F1: all TtRr (tall round). F1 × F1: 16 combinations. F2: 9 T_R_ : 3 T_rr : 3 ttR_ : 1 ttrr = 9:3:3:1 phenotypic ratio. Demonstrates Law of Independent Assortment.

exercises.question: Distinguish between genotype and phenotype.
exercises.answer: Genotype: the actual genetic makeup of an organism (e.g. TT, Tt, tt). Phenotype: the observable physical characteristic determined by genotype (e.g. tall or dwarf). Two organisms with different genotypes (TT and Tt) can have same phenotype (tall).

exercises.question: Why did Mendel choose 7 pairs of contrasting traits?
exercises.answer: Mendel chose traits that: (i) showed clear-cut differences (tall vs dwarf) (ii) were stable over generations (iii) could be easily observed and measured (iv) were inherited independently. He studied one trait at a time (monohybrid) then two (dihybrid) to establish clear ratios.

exercises.question: Explain how genes control traits.
exercises.answer: Genes (segments of DNA) carry information to make specific proteins (enzymes). Enzymes catalyse specific metabolic reactions. Example: genes for tall pea plants produce more enzymes for auxin synthesis → more cell elongation → taller plant. Gene → mRNA → protein → trait.

exercises.question: Distinguish between inherited and acquired traits. Give examples.
exercises.answer: Inherited traits: coded in DNA, passed from parents to offspring. Examples: eye colour, blood group, dimples, height. Acquired traits: developed during lifetime due to environment, use, or learning. Not coded in DNA. Not passed to offspring. Examples: bodybuilder's muscles, ability to speak a language, scar from injury.
